data_flow_pipeline.entry_point: User provides command-line arguments and input files (via CLI or stdin). The main entry point is in build/main.rs which invokes the bat binary. Arguments are parsed by clap, and inputs are specified as file paths or stdin.

stages.responsibility: Parse command-line arguments, validate input sources (files or stdin), and prepare Input/InputReader objects
stages.input: Command-line arguments, file paths, stdin stream
stages.output: Input objects with metadata, InputReader for reading file/stdin content

stages.responsibility: Load highlighting assets (syntax definitions, themes), apply user configuration, detect syntax and theme
stages.input: Input metadata, config options, environment variables
stages.output: HighlightingAssets, selected Theme, detected Syntax, Config with all settings

stages.responsibility: Preprocess file content (expand tabs, handle UTF-16, strip ANSI), detect git diffs if in diff mode, handle lessopen protocol
stages.output: Preprocessed content, git diff metadata (if applicable)

stages.responsibility: Apply syntax highlighting using syntect, parse ANSI escape sequences, generate line decorations, apply style components
stages.output: Highlighted lines with ANSI codes, decoration text

stages.responsibility: Format output for terminal, route to pager or stdout, manage output handle lifecycle
stages.input: Highlighted and formatted lines, paging mode configuration
stages.output: Terminal-ready ANSI-formatted text sent to stdout or pager process

data_flow_pipeline.exit_point: Output is written to stdout (direct or piped to pager like less). The pager displays the formatted, syntax-highlighted content with decorations to the user's terminal.

input.rs.role: Handles user input acquisition and validation. Manages Input objects representing files or stdin, provides InputReader for sequential line/byte reading.

config.rs.role: Central configuration container. Holds all user settings.

assets.rs.role: Manages syntax definitions and color themes. Loads from binary assets or cache, detects syntax by filename/extension/content.

theme.rs.role: Theme selection and color scheme detection.

printer.rs.role: Core output rendering engine. Two printer types: SimplePrinter (plain text), InteractivePrinter (with syntax highlighting, decorations, line numbers).

vscreen.rs.role: Virtual screen and ANSI escape sequence parsing.

decorations.rs.role: Generates line decorations: line numbers, git change markers, grid borders.

controller.rs.role: Main orchestration logic. Coordinates input reading, printer selection, file processing, and output routing.

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller::run_with_error_handler iterates over input files, calls Input::open to open each file and get InputReader
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Input objects -> InputReader for sequential content reading

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller receives Config object with all user settings
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Config struct containing all rendering options

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller passes HighlightingAssets to printer for syntax/theme lookup
Controller::print_input creates InteractivePrinter or SimplePrinter based on config, calls print_header/print_line/print_footer
Controller::run_with_error_handler calls OutputType::from_mode to create appropriate output handle
InteractivePrinter::print_line uses EscapeSequenceIterator to parse ANSI codes and maintain style state
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: ANSI-encoded line content -> parsed escape sequences and text segments

cross_module_interactions.interaction: InteractivePrinter generates line decorations via LineNumberDecoration, LineChangesDecoration, GridBorderDecoration
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Line number, git change status -> formatted decoration text with styling

cross_module_interactions.interaction: InteractivePrinter uses StyleComponents to determine which visual elements to render
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: StyleComponents -> boolean flags for each element

cross_module_interactions.interaction: InteractivePrinter::preprocess calls expand_tabs, replace_nonprintable, strip_ansi, strip_overstrike
InteractivePrinter uses HighlightingAssets to get syntax highlighter and theme colors
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Syntax reference -> highlighted regions with color codes

cross_module_interactions.interaction: HighlightingAssets::get_theme uses theme preference to select theme
HighlightingAssets::get_syntax_for_path consults SyntaxMapping for custom mappings
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: File path -> mapped Syntax or None

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller::print_input calls get_git_diff if diff_mode is enabled
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: File path -> HashMap of line numbers to change status

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller::print_input may call lessopen::open to preprocess files via LESSOPEN protocol
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Input file path -> preprocessed content via external command

cross_module_interactions.interaction: OutputType::try_pager creates Pager instance and spawns pager process
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: PagingMode, WrappingMode -> Pager with stdio pipes

cross_module_interactions.interaction: InteractivePrinter writes formatted lines to OutputHandle

extension_proposal.feature: Add support for custom output formatters (JSON, HTML, Markdown) alongside terminal output
required_changes: output.rs: Add new OutputType variant with corresponding formatter implementations
config.rs: Add output_format field to Config struct
printer.rs: Create trait-based formatter abstraction (Formatter trait)
controller.rs: Route output through appropriate formatter based on config
decorations.rs: Adapt decoration generation to work with multiple output formats
integration_points: Stage 5 (Output Formatting & Paging): Insert formatter selection logic before output handle creation
printer.rs print_line: Use formatter abstraction instead of direct terminal output
output.rs: Extend OutputType enum to support new formats
config.rs: Add CLI argument parsing for --output-format flag
